currentFocus: Static RE/Ghidra closure remains unchanged. Current source-safe standing-wave work added an internal viewer/editor command-boundary proof plan and checker that keep -modelviewer and -cutsceneeditor source/internal and blocked from product launch surfaces. Runtime, rebuild, payload, Ghidra, storage, release, command-arming, and product-enable non-claims remain preserved.

campaignPosture.summary: The prior closeout recorded deleted standing-loop automations. That stop posture is superseded by the 2026-07-02 user request restoring automations. The first restored wave was integrated and pushed at 898e583efdff86847c0a55dca15f2ac088e43fc1; read-only acceptance then required sanitizing exact maintainer-local Ghidra project/backup paths from current tracked runbook wording.
nonClaims: 2026-07-03 storage cleanup performed under explicit user direction

publicPrimaryBoundary: The public repo may track RE docs, wave notes, scratch text exports, function indexes, static maps, proof summaries, checkers, and state batons. Hard payloads, full Ghidra databases/backups, raw captures, copied executables, secrets, build output, package output, local proof paths, raw manifests, and local campaign/worktree paths remain out of tracked state.

rebuildReadiness.sourceBoundary: The checker consumes only tracked public-safe files and validates source continuity, expected counters, front-door links, fail-closed public-boundary wording, and non-claims.
rebuildReadiness.futureGate: A later readiness-gate proof needs a separate bounded assignment and must not reuse this checker as a generic gate without updating the bound source proof and expected counters.

sourceOnlyVocabularyMap.status: context-only planning input with public-source checker
sourceOnlyVocabularyMap.boundary: Source/internal tool names, flags, developer-menu labels, resource tags, and pipeline vocabulary are candidate terms only. Retail binary/save/runtime evidence remains higher authority before any behavior, command, importer, generated-output, runtime, rebuild, or parity claim.
sourceOnlyVocabularyMap.validation: Current checker reconciliation passed self-test, check, npm script, diff whitespace, state JSON parse, docs commands, Markdown links, repo hygiene, hard-payload safety, and public allowlist. The checker validates exact canonical/Lore parity, tracked public Markdown links, required authority-tier and scope-firewall wording, overclaim guards, and bounded tmm-arm4-readiness-gate continuity.

internalViewerEditorBoundary.boundary: -modelviewer and -cutsceneeditor remain source/internal vocabulary and blocked product flags. The plan uses Tier C source vocabulary plus tracked Tier B static command-boundary docs only.
nonClaims: rebuild, visual, gameplay, or no-noticeable-difference proof

loreAndWinuiBoundaries.lore: Lore pack fail-closed hardening is integrated but does not prove release readiness, broad ZIP package probe parity, complete traversal protection, runtime UI proof, or package publication.
loreAndWinuiBoundaries.assetLibrary: Asset Library source tests guard passive path summary and polite live-region scope, not runtime UIA/screen-reader behavior.

storageRetention.futureReviewBands: Medium and low values are non-authorized logical review ceilings only, not reclaimable storage, candidate pools, unique physical-size estimates, urgency signals, or deletion budgets.

nextSteps: Verify git refs after this internal viewer/editor command-boundary slice is pushed before launching another slice.
Keep static RE mutation paused unless a product/runtime contradiction points back to a specific RE issue.
Treat tmm-arm4-readiness-gate as proof-plan/checker support only; do not claim readiness-gate execution, runtime proof, or rebuild parity.
Use PatchBench and Asset Library source tests as bounded source guards only; do not claim live runtime UI/accessibility proof from them.
Do not run live BEA/CDB/audio proof without explicit runtime-proof authority, leases, private proof-root readiness, arm phrase handling, and cleanup validation.
Do not delete future Ghidra/proof material without a candidate-specific local manifest, retained alternative or structured summary, consults where warranted, explicit authority, and acceptance.

Use the source-only internal tooling vocabulary map only to choose one bounded future question with a named higher-authority proof class; keep the internal viewer/editor checker as a public-source boundary guard, not proof authority or command authorization.
